expectations.text: Names SpikeSorting.populate as the immediate next step (not SpikeSortingV1.populate — the actual v1 class is named SpikeSorting)
expectations.evidence: 'in the v1 module the populated table is just called SpikeSorting (not SpikeSortingV1)'; uses `SpikeSorting.populate(key)`.

expectations.text: Gives an ordered next-N list: SpikeSorting.populate → CurationV1 insert → MetricCuration → SpikeSortingOutput merge insert
expectations.evidence: Summary table: 1) SpikeSorting populate, 2) CurationV1.insert_curation, 3) MetricCurationSelection+MetricCuration, 4) SpikeSortingOutput insert.

expectations.text: Notes that CurationV1 comes before MetricCuration in the v1 flow
expectations.evidence: 'CurationV1 must exist before metric curation, because metric curation operates on top of an existing curation'.
